annotations.label: Borel–Weil–Bott theorem (overview)

anchor.snippet: the Borel–Weil–Bott theorem is a basic result in the representation theory of Lie groups

annotations.note: No Borel–Weil–Bott theorem or its sheaf-cohomology apparatus for flag varieties appears in Mathlib (grep for `Borel.Weil`, `BorelWeil`, `Bott` finds nothing relevant).

annotations.label: Setup: G, T, B, integral weight and associated line bundle

anchor.snippet: Let G be a semisimple Lie group or algebraic group

annotations.note: Mathlib has semisimple Lie algebras and root systems but no Borel subgroup, integral weight lattice, or line bundle L_λ on G/B.

annotations.label: Dot action of the Weyl group centered at ρ

anchor.snippet: We first need to describe the Weyl group action centered at

annotations.note: The linear Weyl-group action on a root pairing exists (`RootPairing.weylGroup`), but the ρ-shifted dot action w·λ = w(λ+ρ)−ρ is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Dominant weight

anchor.snippet: a weight μ is said to be dominant

annotations.note: No definition of dominant integral weight (μ with ⟨μ,α∨⟩ ≥ 0 for all simple coroots) exists in Mathlib's root-system or Lie-algebra files.

annotations.label: Dichotomy for integral weights under dot action
annotations.note: The dichotomy (either some w·λ is dominant with trivial stabilizer, or λ is fixed by a nontrivial reflection under the dot action) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Borel–Weil–Bott theorem (statement)

anchor.snippet: The theorem states that in the first case

annotations.note: The precise cohomology-shifting statement H^ℓ(w)(G/B, L_λ) ≅ H^0(G/B, L_{w·λ}) with vanishing in other degrees is absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Characterization of case (1) and recovery of Borel–Weil

anchor.snippet: Case (1) above occurs if and only if

annotations.note: The identification of case (1) with λ dominant integral (w = e) recovering Borel–Weil is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: SL_2(C): sections of L_n as symmetric powers
annotations.note: Mathlib does not have the identification H^0(P^1, O(n)) ≅ Sym^n((C^2)*) as an SL_2(C)-representation.

annotations.label: Representation theory of sl_2 via Riemann sphere

anchor.snippet: This gives us at a stroke the representation theory of

annotations.note: The full classification of finite-dimensional irreducible sl_2-representations via cohomology of line bundles on the Riemann sphere is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Kempf vanishing theorem

anchor.snippet: This is known as the Kempf vanishing theorem

annotations.note: Mathlib has no formalization of Kempf's vanishing theorem (grep for `Kempf` returns no results).

annotations.label: Failure of simplicity in positive characteristic; Mumford's example
annotations.note: Mumford's example demonstrating non-simplicity of H^0(G/B, L_λ) in positive characteristic is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Borel–Weil theorem (overview)

anchor.snippet: The Borel–Weil theorem provides a concrete model for irreducible representations of compact Lie groups

annotations.note: No formalization of the Borel–Weil model for irreducible representations of compact/complex semisimple Lie groups exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Flag variety and equivariant line bundle L_λ

anchor.snippet: Let G be a connected complex semisimple Lie group

annotations.note: The flag variety G/B and the equivariant line bundle L_λ associated to a character of B are not constructed in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Borel–Weil theorem (precise statement)

anchor.snippet: The Borel–Weil theorem states that if

annotations.note: The isomorphism H^0(G/B, L_λ) ≅ V_λ* as G-representations for λ dominant integral is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Character χ_λ and concrete description of sections of L_λ
annotations.note: No construction of the character χ_λ of B or the concrete description of sections of L_λ as B-equivariant holomorphic functions G → C is present.

annotations.label: Action of G on holomorphic sections
annotations.note: The G-action by left translation on B-equivariant holomorphic sections is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: SL(2,C): characters, sections on CP^1 and weight vectors

anchor.snippet: Let G be the complex special linear group

annotations.note: The concrete SL(2,C) computation of characters of the upper-triangular Borel, sections on CP^1, and weight vectors as monomials is not formalized.

annotations.label: Weight vectors as monomials of weight 2i−n

anchor.snippet: Weight vectors are given by monomials

annotations.note: Identification of weight vectors in H^0(CP^1, O(n)) with monomials z^i (weight 2i−n) is not formalized in Mathlib.
